stdout:
**Acceptance Criteria**

Blocking failure:

- REQ-05 is still not met. Generic link labels like `satisfies:<issue>/<self-id>` resolve only through the new command helper [crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs:214), but that helper is not used by the validation/rule paths. `label-coverage` still credits only exact unqualified labels via `l == format!("{satisfies_ns}:{id}")` in [crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:672), so `satisfies:56ab0224/REQ-01` will not satisfy criterion `REQ-01`. `criteria-to-check` likewise checks only exact `<namespace>:<id>` labels in [crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:1361). The repo namespace example also remains unqualified at [.jit/config.toml](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.jit/config.toml:95).

- REQ-02 appears addressed: `seen` is now hoisted across all kinds in [crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:354), with a cross-kind collision test at [crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:505).

- `jit issue show <issue>/<self-id>` appears addressed for the single-id non-projection path in [crates/jit/src/main.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/main.rs:996), with CLI coverage in [crates/jit/tests/item_cli_tests.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/item_cli_tests.rs:212).

**Architecture And Separation**

No new storage-boundary violation found in the item feature. Query orchestration remains in [crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs:35), and projection remains pure in [crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:339).

The unresolved REQ-05 issue is also partly an architecture issue: qualified link resolution was added as a command helper, but the validation domain where link semantics are enforced still has separate string-comparison logic.

**Functional Paradigm**

No blocking issue. The new cross-kind uniqueness logic uses imperative loops in [crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:356), but it is pure and scoped.

**Correctness And Invariants**

No new writes are introduced by item listing/showing, so atomic writes and event logging are not directly affected. Gate semantics, assignee format, labels format, and PID safety are unchanged by this patch.

Blocking correctness issue remains: qualified item references do not satisfy the existing validation rules that define coverage/check semantics.

**Safety**

`#![deny(unsafe_code)]` remains in place. I found no unsafe code introduced by the changed files.

**Testing**

I did not run tests because the issue has a succeeded `cargo-ci` gate in the provided context (`passed`, 2026-06-25 13:36:48 UTC).

Coverage gap: there is still no validation test proving `label-coverage` accepts `satisfies:<issue>/<self-id>` as coverage. The added tests only prove `CommandExecutor::resolve_link_label` resolves qualified labels, not that the gate/rule engine uses them.

**Error Handling**

The prior JSON error issue for `jit item ... --json` appears addressed in [crates/jit/src/main.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/main.rs:423), with CLI coverage at [crates/jit/tests/item_cli_tests.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/item_cli_tests.rs:249).

**CLI Contract**

Success and failure JSON for item commands are covered. `issue show <qualified-id> --json` is also handled. No new CLI-contract blocker beyond the REQ-05 validation semantics gap.

**Documentation**

Public comments claim link namespaces reference qualified ids, e.g. [crates/jit/src/config.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/config.rs:243), but the live validation behavior still expects unqualified values. That mismatch is technical debt and supports the REQ-05 failure.

**Prior Review Feedback**

Most prior findings were addressed: REQ-02, `issue show <issue>/<self-id>`, and JSON error output. The prior REQ-05 finding is unresolved because qualified link labels are still not accepted by `label-coverage` / `criteria-to-check`.

**Jit Issue Management**

The listed dependency `5f5735c3` is marked done. Provided gate evidence shows `cargo-ci` passed and prior `code-review` failed; this review still fails due to REQ-05.

VERDICT: FAIL
